statcast_watch: Foster Griffin is set to start for Washington Nationals versus Pittsburgh Pirates at 12:35 PM ET, and his recent Statcast sample covers 340 pitches, giving this matchup a measurable pitching signal to watch.

Braxton Ashcraft gets the ball for Pittsburgh Pirates against Washington Nationals at 12:35 PM ET and has touched 99.1 mph; has carried an average spin rate around 2497, making him one of the stronger Statcast pitching signals on tonight’s board.

Landen Roupp gets the ball for San Francisco Giants against Cincinnati Reds at 12:40 PM ET and has generated a healthy 24.8% whiff rate on swings, making him one of the stronger Statcast pitching signals on tonight’s board.
matchup_flags: Washington Nationals at Pittsburgh Pirates (12:35 PM ET) features a listed starters matchup of Foster Griffin vs. Braxton Ashcraft, giving the game a strong pregame pitching frame.
San Francisco Giants at Cincinnati Reds (12:40 PM ET) draws attention with Landen Roupp lined up against Chase Burns, making starting pitching the clearest early storyline.
Kansas City Royals at Detroit Tigers (1:10 PM ET) stands out behind a probable starters matchup of Kris Bubic and Keider Montero.
board_context: A full MLB board remains in play, putting extra emphasis on probable pitchers and pregame matchup edges.

league_efficiency_watch: League-wide advanced metrics were not fully available in this report window, but net rating, pace, and shooting efficiency remain the primary evaluation anchors.
why_it_matters: Net rating helps separate sustainable team strength from surface-level record noise.
Pace matters because it shapes possession volume, transition pressure, and live scoring environment.
True shooting percentage offers a cleaner read on shot-quality conversion than raw field goal percentage alone.
Turnover rate and defensive rating help identify which teams are more likely to survive tighter postseason-style possessions.
story_angles: Playoff-context framing: pace, shot efficiency, and turnover control become more useful editorial signals as the postseason environment tightens.
